channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iPhone 17 Price Increases Expected in 2025: Industry Analyst
item.description: Industry analyst predicts price increase for iPhone 17, expected to launch in September 2025. Pricing details and industry trends explored.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online ‘constantly,’ Pew report finds
item.description: YouTube was the single most popular platform teenagers used — 90% said they watched videos on the site, down slightly from 95% in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Feds propose protection for giant salamanders devastated by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A giant salamander that evolved on the supercontinent Pangaea and outlived the dinosaurs has been proposed for federal protection by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chicago flunks cloud economics
item.description: The city of Chicago has devised a brilliant plan to jack up its cloud computing tax from 9% to 11%. As someone who has worked in tech for decades, I’ve seen this movie before, and (spoiler alert) it didn’t have a happy ending. This is not a diatribe against taxes; taxes are not the issue here.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump’s pick of David Sacks as AI and crypto czar shows how cozy his White House will be with Silicon Valley
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s approach to crypto is becoming clearer with the appointment of entrepreneur David Sacks as Trump’s “White House A.I. & Crypto Czar”—signaling a shift in how the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: 12 easy, techy ways to make the holiday smoother
item.description: To make the busiest time of year more manageable, here are some tricks for tracking your packages, taking quality family photos and curating the perfect Christmas playlist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Global internet traffic surges 17% as post-quantum cryptography adoption grows
item.description: Overall internet traffic continues to grow year after year, with no slowdown in sight. Cloudflare released its annual Cloudflare Radar Year in Review report this week, providing insights into the state of the internet in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Azure hardware innovations and the serverless cloud future
item.description: Microsoft uses its events to lift the hood on Azure’s hardware and the infrastructure it uses to manage and run its services. Ignite 2024 was no different.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI in the workplace is forcing younger tech workers to rethink their career paths
item.description: Entry-level workers increasingly fear job loss or significant changes to their careers due to automation made possible by generative AI (genAI).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Android 2025 preview: 7 things to watch
item.description: As we look ahead to a whole new year of green-tinted Googley goodness, I’m feeling both guardedly excited — and curiously like Bill Murray in the movie Groundhog Day. Google’s got some pretty weighty pivots in the works for the Android arena right now.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenSilver 3.1 brings XAML designer for VS Code
item.description: OpenSilver 3.1, the latest version of Userware’s open-source framework for cross-platform .NET development, has arrived with what the company calls the first-ever drag-and-drop XAML designer for Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The makers and takers of WordPress
item.description: The idea of open-source software seems kind of nuts. Millions (billions?) of lines of code doing all kinds of amazing things and available for free? That sounds too good to be true. But it is true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Goldilocks spot for one startup founder? Right between the business and tech teams
item.description: For Brian Jacobs, CEO of weElevateIT, bringing together different DoD teams to implement the latest technology and modernize is his idea of the perfect job. The post The Goldilocks spot for one startup founder? Right between the business and tech teams first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to build better AI chatbots
item.description: At Campfire, we launched a consumer AI chat product called Cozy Friends on Steam and mobile on October 12. Imagine Animal Crossing or The Sims with AI agents. In the first 30 days, users exchanged more than 1.7M messages with our AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China launches anti-monopoly probe into Nvidia amid rising US-China chip tensions
item.description: China has initiated an investigation into Nvidia over alleged violations of the country’s anti-monopoly laws, signaling a potential escalation in the ongoing tech and trade tensions between Beijing and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
